gate_note: READ-ONLY UPSTREAM PIN. This triad pins the stock, non-Material-guarded svtools TabBar/TabDrawer base symbols that ScTabControl (and three other application subclasses) delegate their sheet-tab-strip geometry to. Per the inventory legend, existing upstream source that is not behind a Material guard NEVER satisfies the M gate, so advances_m_gate is false: this row proves Batch A's calc-sheet-tabs.json base_call premise (ScTabControl additively overlays, never forks, the shared TabBar geometry) and must NOT be reported as advancing WIN-CA-004's M gate. The Material accent-strip overlay that DOES advance M lives in the separate, guarded qa/windows-ui-contract/calc-sheet-tabs.json.

upstream_files.note: Shared svtools TabBar/TabBarPageBits declaration; four application subclasses consume it, so this row pins it read-only and never edits it.
Shared svtools TabBar/TabDrawer paint + geometry implementation; the sheet-tab strip band, docked-tab fills, Add-Sheet button and scroll buttons are all drawn here, not in sc/.

symbols.role: The shared base class ScTabControl extends; all sheet-tab geometry originates here.

symbols.symbol: virtual void Paint( vcl::RenderContext& rRenderContext, const tools::Rectangle& rRect ) override;
symbols.role: The virtual Paint the ScTabControl override calls up into (Batch A's calc-sheet-tabs.json base_call marker delegates to TabBar::Paint).
Pre-paint geometry pass that lays out the docked tabs before the strip is drawn.
The Add-Sheet button hook; the '+' affordance is a base-class control, not a Calc-owned surface.
Per-tab rectangle geometry the strip layout depends on.
The base-class helper that paints an individual docked tab (outer frame, text, color line, separator).
TabDrawer's per-tab draw entry point invoked from TabBar::Paint.

symbols.symbol: void TabBar::Paint(vcl::RenderContext& rRenderContext, const tools::Rectangle& rect)
symbols.role: The concrete base paint the ScTabControl override delegates to; renders the full strip band and every docked tab.

subclasses.note: Calc's own sheet-tab strip; carries the guarded Material accent overlay pinned by calc-sheet-tabs.json.
Basic IDE module tab bar; shares the identical svtools base, so an edit to the base would ripple here (Batch C is actively touching bastypes).
Impress slide/view tab control; shares the identical svtools base.

subclasses.symbol: class SAL_DLLPUBLIC_RTTI LayerTabBar final : public TabBar,
subclasses.note: Draw layer tab bar; shares the identical svtools base.

notes: Read-only verification only. It proves the base_call premise of Batch A's calc-sheet-tabs.json (ScTabControl delegates its full sheet-tab geometry to the shared svtools TabBar/TabDrawer, additively overlaying the Material accent strip rather than forking geometry) and records the four-subclass shared-ownership fact so a future edit to svtools/tabbar cannot be made in the belief that it only affects Calc. It grants Calc no edit rights over svtools. Source evidence only: runtime_verified is false and advances_m_gate is false throughout; no native build, tab pixels, or runtime interaction are claimed. Deferred, not in this slice: ScAutoCalculateControl (sc/source/ui/app/acctrl.cxx, FID_AUTO_CALC) is the one genuinely Calc-owned, owner-drawn, currently-unguarded status control, but it has zero design-chapter coverage so a source guard cannot honestly be written for it until a 05-navigation.md status-bar note lands; folding the plain-text status fields (SID_STATUS_DOCPOS/SID_STATUS_SUM/SID_ROWCOL_SELCOUNT) and ScProgress into the generic WIN-NAV-008 band is a documentation closure fact, not a new surface.
